PP.sub: Oversupplied as plants run flat out
bullets: NSRP, BSR and Hyosung Vina all at 100% capacity; buyers indifferent to imports.
Lotte copo offered 1,400-1,450 CIF with bids well below; IRPC homo at 1,260 CFR.
Platts SEA -60: HDPE 1,230-1,240, LLDPE 1,190, LDPE 1,440 CFR.
Petrochina/Sinopec LLDPE settled 1,070; HDPE settlements 1,120-1,170 CIF.
Prefchem Malaysia restart late June threatens more supply; off-season into August.

PVC.sub: TPC Vina leads sharp July cuts
bullets: TPC Vina cut SG660/SP660 by 3,000 to 20,000 ex-VAT (~21,600-22,032 incl VAT).
AGC Vina PM66R to 19,700-20,000 ex-VAT; large buyers may bid ~19,500.
Import AGC K66 down another 20 to 730-740 CIF; China PVC -10 to 750 CFR.

editorial.changedNote: 13 of 50 moved (12 down, 1 up), led by PVC

summary: Platts: NEA PE down (HDPE -20, LLDPE -30, LDPE -40); SEA steeper at -60, HDPE to 1,230-1,240, LLDPE 1,190, LDPE 1,440 CFR.
Crude's sharp fall and weak demand drove the drop; Petrochina/Sinopec LLDPE settled 1,070, HDPE settlements 1,120-1,170 CIF.
Prefchem Malaysia restart late June adds supply; domestic LLDPE deals near 33,000, Chinese grades the floor.
paras: According to Platts assessments published today (18 June), PE prices in Northeast Asia fell this week, with HDPE Film, yarn, injection and blow grades all down 20 USD/ton week-on-week, LLDPE down 30 USD/ton and LDPE down 40 USD/ton. In Southeast Asia the declines were steeper: all HDPE grades fell 60 USD/ton to 1,230-1,240 USD/ton CFR, LLDPE fell 60 USD/ton to 1,190 USD/ton CFR, LLDPE metallocene C6 fell 60 USD/ton to 1,340 USD/ton CFR, and LDPE fell 60 USD/ton to 1,440 USD/ton CFR.

The Asian PE market dropped fairly sharply this week on the back of a steep fall in crude oil and limited demand. Competition is especially intense in Southeast Asia given abundant supply — alongside Chinese material, numerous offers are coming from South Korea and within Southeast Asia. Notably, Prefchem's PE complex in Malaysia is expected to restart in late June, which could add further supply pressure.

In Vietnam, sources report falling transaction volumes for HDPE and LLDPE this week as cheaper Chinese offers continue to dominate the market, while some deals were recorded for Korean-origin LDPE. In the short term, demand is expected to stay low, with the off-season likely to last until August.

Today, Petrochina's LLDPE Film 7042/7042N settled with Vietnamese buyers at a low of 1,070 USD/ton CIF, 30 USD/ton below the offer at the start of the week. Sinopec likewise settled LLDPE Film into Vietnam at 1,070 USD/ton CIF and offered LLDPE injection at 1,130 USD/ton CIF, while Resources offered LLDPE injection at 1,180-1,200 USD/ton CIF.

On HDPE, Sinopec offered HDPE Film into Vietnam at 1,230 USD/ton CIF early in the week, but by mid-week a buyer disclosed a possible settlement at 1,120 USD/ton CIF — 110 USD/ton below the offer, though this level may not be widespread. Similarly, BASF's (China) HDPE Film 6095 was offered at 1,225 USD/ton CIF early in the week, with a distributor later accepting settlement at 1,170 USD/ton CIF, 55 USD/ton below the offer; HDPE Film from STL and Baofeng was offered at 1,230-1,240 USD/ton CIF. In HDPE blow, one distributor reported settling US-origin Cynpol 5502H with Vietnamese buyers at 1,150 USD/ton CIF, 40 USD/ton below the prior week's offer, and US-origin Tricon 5502 was also offered at 1,150 USD/ton CIF — a level no longer very attractive given long US shipping times, as some fast-delivery Chinese material is already priced at 1,150-1,200 USD/ton CIF.

Domestically, LSP has nearly cleared its PP and PE inventory for the local market after a month-long shutdown, largely on the back of stepped-up exports. This week LSP had only a small volume of HDPE blow, offered at a high 38,800 VND/kg (VAT inclusive) in the south and 39,500 VND/kg (VAT inclusive) in the north — levels that have barely transacted. Some commercial sellers still holding LSP PE material are settling at much lower prices, and LSP's distribution office is additionally offering Chinese-origin HDPE.

Price spreads between distributors remain wide. Mid-week, one distributor offered SK Chemical (Korea) HDPE yarn 3301 at 36,500 VND/kg (VAT inclusive) and Lotte Hyundai (Korea) HDPE yarn MF5000 at 37,500 VND/kg (VAT inclusive), down 500 VND/kg from late last week. Middle East-origin HDPE Film was commonly offered around 37,000-38,000 VND/kg (VAT inclusive); notably, a large customer reported importing STL (China) HDPE FS7000 at just 34,800 VND/kg (VAT inclusive), though most smaller customers were still being offered around 36,000 VND/kg (VAT inclusive).

For LDPE, a leading distributor was offering Lotte Titan (Malaysia) LDPE Film 260GG at 45,000 VND/kg (VAT inclusive), below most other traders' offers, while Petrochina (China) LDPE 2426H was commonly offered at 44,000-44,500 VND/kg (VAT inclusive), with some sellers dumping at just 40,000-40,500 VND/kg (VAT inclusive). For LLDPE, even premium Middle East grades such as Sabic 218WJ and Qamar FD21HS changed hands today at 35,000 VND/kg (VAT inclusive); Chinese-origin LLDPE Film was offered by one seller at 34,500 VND/kg (VAT inclusive), but transactions since last week have been recorded around 33,000 VND/kg (VAT inclusive) or lower.

summary: Platts: NEA PP -70 (block copo -130); SEA milder, yarn/inj -20 to 1,180, BOPP -20 to 1,200, block copo +20 to 1,230 CFR.
US-Iran peace pulled crude lower; NSRP, BSR and Hyosung Vina all at 100% rates, leaving the market oversupplied.
Lotte copo 1,400-1,450 (bids well below); IRPC homo 1,260; domestic 114G 32,800-33,000, T3034 33,800-34,000.
paras: According to Platts assessments, PP prices in Northeast Asia fell this week, with PP yarn, injection and IPP grades down 70 USD/ton week-on-week, BOPP down 50 USD/ton, and PP block copolymer down sharply by 130 USD/ton. Declines in Southeast Asia were smaller, following two prior weeks of steep cuts: PP yarn/injection fell 20 USD/ton to 1,180 USD/ton CFR, BOPP fell 20 USD/ton to 1,200 USD/ton CFR, and IPP Film fell 40 USD/ton to 1,230 USD/ton CFR, while PP block copolymer edged up 20 USD/ton to 1,230 USD/ton CFR after a particularly sharp drop the previous week.

PP prices across Asia weakened this week on the back of an improved outlook in the Middle East, where a US-Iran peace agreement pulled crude oil sharply lower. Supply is also improving as several Asian plants restarted earlier than expected following maintenance.

In Vietnam, apart from LSP, which has shut down and temporarily halted production, the remaining PP plants are running at high rates — NSRP, BSR and Hyosung Vina are all reportedly operating at 100% of capacity, resulting in oversupply. This has left buyers largely indifferent to imported material, particularly in the PP homopolymer segment.

Today (18 June), the distributor of Lotte Chemical (South Korea) offered PP copolymer to Vietnam this week at 1,400-1,450 USD/ton CIF depending on grade — a level considered quite high, with buyers bidding well below it. Earlier in the week, Lotte offered coated-grade PP (PP tráng) to Vietnam at a high 1,500 USD/ton CIF and PP film at 1,330 USD/ton CIF, while its PP yarn was reported to have closed at 1,170 USD/ton CIF.

Mid-week, the distributor of IRPC (Thailand) also offered PP and PS for July 2026 shipment to Vietnam. IRPC's PP offers fell sharply by 90-120 USD/ton from early June (for June-July 2026 delivery), bringing PP homopolymer down to 1,260 USD/ton CFR Ho Chi Minh City, PP Film 1126NK to 1,350 USD/ton CFR, PP 1100RC to 1,310 USD/ton CFR, and PP B1101 extrusion to 1,370 USD/ton CFR.

In the domestic market, the lowest offer for NSRP's PP 114G was recorded at 32,800-33,000 VND/kg (VAT inclusive), while in the south BSR's PP T3034 traded at 33,800-34,000 VND/kg (VAT inclusive). Among other grades in the north, sellers offered US-origin PP copolymer (off-grade/near-prime) at 31,000-33,000 VND/kg (VAT inclusive) depending on grade and producer, Hanwha (Korea) PP copolymer BJ350 and ExxonMobil PP copolymer 7032E3 at 37,000 VND/kg (VAT inclusive), and TPC Singapore PP Random YR5053 at the same 37,000 VND/kg (VAT inclusive).

In the south, PP copolymer offers commonly ranged 36,000-38,000 VND/kg (VAT inclusive). Lotte (Korea) coated PP M9600 ran at 46,000-47,000 VND/kg (VAT inclusive), while PP Film of Korean, Indian and Thai origin was generally quoted at 41,000-42,000 VND/kg (VAT inclusive), down from the previous week.

summary: Platts: China PVC -10 to 750, SEA -35 to 725 CFR; high inventories and weak demand keep pressure on sellers.
TPC Vina's July offer cut SG660/SP660 by 3,000 to 20,000 ex-VAT (21,600-22,032 incl VAT) for large buyers.
AGC Vina PM66R cut to 19,700-20,000 ex-VAT; import AGC K66 down another 20 to 730-740 CIF.
paras: This week, China's overall PVC operating rate was recorded at around 69.47%, up 1.68 percentage points from the previous week. Of this, the operating rate for carbide-based PVC rose 0.95 percentage points week-on-week to 77.62%, while ethylene-based PVC climbed 3.37 percentage points to 50.27%. In South Korea, Hanwha Solutions has cut its PVC operating rate — covering both suspension and paste resin — from around 80% to 60-70%, effective since late last week.

On pricing, according to Platts assessments, Asian PVC prices continued to decline this week amid weak demand and high inventories. PVC prices in China fell 10 USD/ton week-on-week to 750 USD/ton CFR, while prices in Southeast Asia dropped 35 USD/ton to 725 USD/ton CFR. With inventories running high, buyers are in no hurry to purchase, and suppliers are facing considerable selling pressure.

In Vietnam, today (18 June), TPC Vina announced its July 2026 PVC offer prices for the domestic market, with sharp reductions from the previous month. Specifically, TPC Vina's SG660/SP660 offer to large customers this month was cut sharply by 3,000 VND/kg to 20,000 VND/kg (excluding VAT); offers to medium and small customers stood at 20,200-20,400 VND/kg (excluding VAT). Including 8% VAT, the offers translate to 21,600-22,032 VND/kg (VAT inclusive).

Earlier this week, AGC Vina (ACVN) announced its July 2026 domestic PVC offer prices. Its PM66R grade was offered at 20,400-20,600 VND/kg (excluding VAT) to medium and small customers, and at 20,200 VND/kg (excluding VAT) to its largest customers — a sharp reduction of 2,500-2,800 VND/kg. Today, distributors lowered their offers further to 19,700-20,000 VND/kg (excluding VAT), down 600 VND/kg from the start of the week. Large customers may even bid at around 19,500 VND/kg (VAT inclusive).

In the trade market, a leading distributor is still offering Yihua's SG-5 PVC at 21,500 VND/kg (VAT inclusive) in Yen Vien (Hanoi). Meanwhile, ethylene-based PVC continued to see sharp price cuts from many sellers, bringing the prevailing price down to around 22,500-23,000 VND/kg (VAT inclusive) — only about 1,000 VND/kg higher than carbide-based PVC. One distributor even offered AGC Vinythai Thailand's 266RC grade (ethylene-based) at 22,200 VND/kg (VAT inclusive), competing strongly with ethylene-based PVC from other origins.

On the import front, last week AGC Group offered K66 PVC for July 2026 shipment to Vietnam from its two plants — Vinythai Thailand (AVT) and Asahimas Indonesia (ASC) — at 750-760 USD/ton CIF depending on volume (with volumes above 1,000 tons reported at 740 USD/ton CIF). This week, however, the distributor announced an additional discount of 20 USD/ton on all K66 and K58 grades for customers purchasing 500 tons or more. As a result, AGC's PVC selling price this month has fallen to 730-740 USD/ton CIF, although few customers are currently willing to commit to orders of 500 tons or more.
